title: Domain Evidence, Regulation, Standards, and Licensing Boundaries · Stakeholder Workshop
summary: A guided product team extends domain authority and compliance boundary dossier while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of domain authority and compliance boundary dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.
Record version, status, jurisdiction, applicability, review date, supersession, and monitoring owner for governing sources.
Identify decisions requiring legal, safety, clinical, financial, accessibility, security, privacy, or domain expert review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Authority Hierarchy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Authority Hierarchy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Authority Hierarchy, keep Authority Hierarchy connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Secondary summaries, a draft framework, incompatible content licenses, and a broad compliance claim are treated as equal governing authority. The workshop variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Distinguish law, regulation, standard, specification, official guidance, contract, policy, research, convention, and opinion by jurisdiction and authority.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Authority Hierarchy condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — Controls perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust product requirements and distinguishes automated checks from human conformance evaluation.
